title: Your Initial Familiarity with NGE
wikitext:
[]: Your Initial Familiarity with NGE.             Quote Post by Trajan » Fri Jan 21, 2011 8:08 pm I've often heard jokes where people say they don't want to spoil the series for the two people who haven't seen it, and that left me wondering: the first time you watched Eva, where you already aware of all the major plot twists? Also, how familiar with the series where you when you first watched?

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Fri Jan 21, 2011 8:20 pm I remember knowing that it was Lilith on the cross, not Adam, but I was surprised when I found out Rei was a clone of Yui. I also knew how the series ended, because I saw 24-26 before watching most of the series. I don't think I knew much else.

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Fri Jan 21, 2011 8:21 pm I love talking about my first time. Well I didn't know crap about the series when I first watched it. I was only aware about it. People had told me it was good. Well one day I was wandering around an FYE and found a cheap used DVD. I bought it, watched it, and I was hooked. The rest is history. (History: Bought death and rebirth a few months later, rented EoE a few months after that, found a boxed set and bought that at the start of 2009, bought EoE to complete my collection. Still buying eva shit to this day. I'm hooked on it like crack.)

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Fri Jan 21, 2011 8:33 pm ayy

[]:             Quote Post by Norsehound » Fri Jan 21, 2011 9:17 pm I think my first exposure to Evangelion was seeing this image in a magazine. I knew little about it except the title. My mind immediately constructed a first impression of the plot: this main character was somehow a magic girl, who rode powerful motorcycle-things fighting aliens (or something) and were somehow influenced/controlled/commanded by/were plotting to rebel against giant monsters... of which there is one in the background. Seeing a model kit of Evangelion 01 making hideous (berserker) faces only reinforced my confusion. Then I actually watched the anime and it took something like five years for me to understand it beyond a mere super robot show. Someday I want to write fanfiction based on my initial guesses on what the premise of the show is like. The Eva one would be... interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by The Abhorrent » Fri Jan 21, 2011 9:21 pm I knew next to nothing about it, only that it was one of the more famous giant robot shows. So just about every single twist in the plot came as a surprise, and quite a few of the more subtle oddities stood out as well. The only thing I was made aware of was that the last two episodes were weird (probably the best spoiler-free description one can get about them), a detail a friend of mine told me when he became aware that I was watching the series. Not too sure, but that may had helped me appreciate those last two episodes a bit more (due to not being shocked by the nature of those episodes. Heck, I actually loved how it played out in the end.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Fri Jan 21, 2011 9:40 pm I watched EoE before the series. And then, for some reason, ended up seeing ep 08 first when starting with series. This happened in -98 or -99... Luckily, I was quite tired when I saw EoE first time and it was from a nth gen VHS fansub, so I didn't really remember (or understand) anything from it... except Asuka's fate and the fact that there's something complex going on between Shinji and Asuka. That kinda colored my view on the series, admittedly...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jan 21, 2011 9:42 pm I'm fairly certain this thread has existed before in many forms.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Fri Jan 21, 2011 10:07 pm NemZ wrote: I'm fairly certain this thread has existed before in many forms.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Jan 21, 2011 10:59 pm I had first discovered it on Adult Swim when it aired. The most that was ever spoiled for me was when I'd watch episode 02, miss a few weeks and pick it up in episode 19 or something. To be honest, episode 21 and EoE had been the only episodes I hadn't seen before joining these forums.

[]:             Quote Post by The Lincolnshire Poacher » Fri Jan 21, 2011 11:35 pm I knew basically nothing. When I was still in middle school, back in the time that Hollywood Video supplied its movies in VHS format, I rented the first volume containing episodes 1-4 as my interest in anime was beginning its phase. I liked what I saw, but didn't bother researching the show afterward. In the time between then, and the moment I blind-bought the Perfect Collection set, I stumbled unto spoilers several times, in random places: 1.) Before I heard of End of Evangelion, I found an AMV featuring the movie set to music by Rammstein, after video-searching on AltaVista (Another dead artifact from my past, like VHS, for context). However, the spoilers didn't imprint on me, and I didn't even realize the accident until after I eventually saw End of Evangelion. 2.) In a magazine dedicated to anime, this image this image was displayed on a page. Once again, I only realized this after I saw the series. 3.) Lastly, I found a website dedicated to posting screenshots of every scene of fanservice from the show. This didn't imprint - except for one image of Shini's hand on Rei's breast... so the first time I watched the Perfect Collection, the expectation of this scene was lingering constantly in the back of my head. Luckily, the anticipation didn't last long, since as I mentioned I already saw episodes 1-4, and the scene happened in episode 5.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Fri Jan 21, 2011 11:47 pm Speaking of movie spoilers, the first time I saw a non AMV video of Eva was the scene where Asuka gets eaten by the Eva Series so her death was already a foregone conclusion. Initially I thought she was a villain of some sort and the Eva Series were the Angels. The first AMV I ever saw was an Eva parody of the Iron Man trailer Tony Stark's dialogue imposed onto Gendo. Weird huh?

[]:             Quote Post by Born of Lilith » Sat Jan 22, 2011 1:59 am All I knew about Evangelion before I watched it was 1. it had Christian symbolism which appealed to my 14 year old self, 2. it had a girl named Rei in it who was awesome and hot and 3. it looked really damn cool.

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Sat Jan 22, 2011 2:38 am I knew or though that or had been told that: 1. It was a series about humanity going against its creator (or God) 2. It ended in the Apocalypse 3. I remember friends were watching episode 5 or 6 in their laptops one time 4. I had heard "Komm Susser Tod" after a friend kept singing it while we were studying math 5. I knew it had some Biblical stuff going on. And that's it, when I first saw it was expecting it to be all mythological and biblical, but since it did take a while to get there, I was a bit disappointed with the first 13 episodes or so upon first viewing. I really had no idea how in the world a show about giant robots fighting aliens was going to end up in some sort of biblical and apocalyptic mess. I, of course, later found out how.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jan 22, 2011 4:17 am I went into it knowing that it had the reputation of being one of the best giant robot anime, and that was it apart from what was on the DVD sleeves -- since I was picking the disks up from the World Cinema section of the local Borders. During the process I somehow discovered that the last couple of episodes were controversial and there was a movie that re-did them. And I got spoiled for that to the extent of the synopsis on the Amazon page. It wasn't until a while after that I realized that I'd read the first chapter of the manga in some Dark Horse publication about 5 years before, and not been impressed.

[]:             Quote Post by CommieGuy » Sat Jan 22, 2011 9:09 am I knew that it had a complex story, but I thought it was like "ooh, the guy who seems to be pure good suddenly betrays everyone and destroys the world". I also knew that Shinji would get raped by Sachiel. Didn't know that he would go berserk though, so I don't think it really matters. If it counts, I already knew most of EoE before watching it. But I had already watched the entire series, so I don't think it does.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jan 22, 2011 10:02 am I was attending an anime party at a friend's house, and we started watching Nadia: The Secret of Blue Water . My friend said "If any of these characters look kinda familiar, it's because this was the show GIANAX did right before their more popular show Evangelion; their characters look awfully similar to one another." That meant nothing to me at the time, but I liked the character designs of Nadia. So some time later I started watching Evangelion over YouTube. That's also when I figured this was the show Little Kuriboh was parodying in his Abridged 101 video. I had absolutely no idea what the story was like. I still wasn't that much into anime at the time either. (My friend liked anime, and he and I had some very good conversations about film before I found out he was into anime, so I was trusting a lot of his judgment at first.) So I wasn't into this whole robot genre stuff that much either, so NGE didn't impress me much until they started explicitly showing that it wasn't a robot. There was also this red-head that kept flashing on the screen during the opening titles that wasn't introduced in the show yet, and knowing Anno's history with wild red-head characters I patiently waited for her to someday be introduced into the show. Boy, was she a blast to watch interact with that shy kid when episode 8 rolled around! Then, like, episode 13 came, and all of this weird stuff starts happening and these unexpected horrible and strange things started happening to the characters, and it wasn't at all charming like Nadia or fun like the first few Evangelion episodes.... and I just couldn't take my eyes off of it! So basically I got into it because of Yoshiyuki Sadamoto and stayed because of Anno.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sat Jan 22, 2011 11:21 am I was very familiar with a lot of the plot twists because I read the manga (up to volume 12) before watching the anime. Excluding NGETV end and EoE of course. Bleh.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sat Jan 22, 2011 2:44 pm I was just getting past my "animu is like Dragon Ball" stage after watching stuff like Cowboy Bebop and Berserk when i read about a show that Wikipedia described, in a paraphrased nutshell: "It starts out stupid, but then it gets deep." I became interested. I didn't even think it started out that stupid, and got hooked early on because of the feeling of thrashy-yet-refined style the show oozed and the impecable directing.

[]:             Quote Post by Ili3kca3k » Sat Jan 22, 2011 9:05 pm I had the feeling Rei was some weird clone type thing and that the Eva's were some how connected to the Angles. With most of the rest of the plot twists... I was like... Really? Didn't expect that. Well except that everyone had pretty much went insane by the end of the series.
